title: Right to Remain Silent & Arrest Protocol
summary: You are not obligated to answer questions beyond state mandatory identity verification.
content:
1. Silence: You have the right to remain silent. State clearly: 'I am exercising my right to remain silent and I want to speak to a lawyer.'
2. Mandatory Info: In most jurisdictions, you only need to provide your full legal name and address if lawfully detained.
3. Detention Check: Ask clearly: 'Am I free to leave?' If police say yes, walk away calmly. If no, you are detained.

title: Phone & Digital Device Seizure Rights
summary: Police cannot force you to unlock your phone without a valid judicial warrant in most legal frameworks.
content:
1. Passcode vs Biometrics: Biometric unlock (Fingerprint/FaceID) can sometimes be compelled by court order; strong numeric passcodes offer higher legal protection.
2. Consent: Explicitly state: 'I do not consent to a search of my phone or device.'
3. Recording Police: You have the right to film police officers in public spaces as long as you do not physically obstruct their operations.

title: Right to Legal Counsel & Medical Aid
summary: Immediate actions upon arrest or custody.
content:
1. Legal Call: You have the right to make a phone call to a legal counsel or designated emergency contact.
2. Medical Care: If injured during arrest or custody, request medical assistance immediately. Officers have a legal duty of care.
